title: Regression or instrumentation review case for skill research
goal: Use one narrow instrumentation/regression candidate to scaffold a real Phase-2A proof path without overstating evidence.

why_this_case: Lower-risk than a routing-default or champion-promotion case.
Targets a concrete reviewable object: skill research with missing task_type classification and apparent 0% success rate.
Improves trigger quality and measurement discipline even if no adoption follows.

signal.summary: Skill research appears as a regression/instrumentation candidate due to missing task_type classification and apparent 0% success rate.
signal.qualification_reason: Selected as the best next real review case because it is diagnostic, reversible, and directly relevant to trigger quality.

review.rationale: The review should test whether the observed alert reflects a true regression or mostly an instrumentation/segmentation problem.

adoption.expected_effect: Cleaner classification of research outcomes and better distinction between genuine regression and measurement artifact.
adoption.rollback_plan: Revert the measurement/segmentation change if it degrades comparability or introduces new ambiguity.

measurement.measurement_method: bounded pre/post observation with explicit caveats

limitations: This is a scaffold, not a completed proof.
The current candidate may resolve into a measurement artifact rather than a genuine regression.

missing_data_points: raw metric snapshot or dashboard reference for the triggering alert
exact sessions/tasks underlying the apparent regression
precise instrumentation or segmentation change proposal
pre/post comparison window and minimum evidence threshold
acceptance_criteria: All five trace-spine objects are instantiated with non-placeholder owner/time fields.
One reversible diagnostic change is actually implemented.
The measurement section states both result and uncertainty.
The final verdict can say whether the case was a true regression, a measurement artifact, or still inconclusive.
